reasons_to_visit.details: The signature mid-August fireworks display over the harbour — one of Europe's largest pyrotechnic shows — anchors days of food markets, concerts, and lakefront installations.
Switzerland's national holiday brings parades, alpenhorn performances, brunch on lake-facing terraces, and fireworks at neighbouring villages around the lake.
With UN, watch industry, and other business travel paused for summer holidays, weekday hotel rates ease meaningfully — one of the best urban value windows of the year.
Lake Geneva peaks at 23–24°C — comfortable extended swimming. All public baths and beaches at full throttle.
Stable late-summer high pressure produces some of the clearest mountain panoramas of the year — perfect for Salève, Mont Blanc, and Jura day trips.
OpenAir Cinema and final Musiques en été nights continue across August evenings.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Late-summer heat domes are increasingly common. Many Geneva hotels lack robust AC; check before booking.
On fireworks weekend, the lakefront is shoulder-to-shoulder for kilometres. Restaurants near the harbour fully booked; bring patience.
Many small artisan shops, family bakeries, and bistros take 2–3 weeks of holidays in August — especially in Carouge and the Old Town.
Persistent heat triggers powerful afternoon thunderstorms; lake activities and outdoor concerts may cancel.
In recent years, southerly winds have occasionally pushed wildfire smoke into the Léman basin.
August Saturdays are notorious for French motorway gridlock and Geneva airport congestion.

weather.precipitation: Hot and largely dry, with thunderstorm clusters. Lake at 23–24°C.
weather.general_tips: Hydrate; protect against sun and storms. Book Fêtes de Genève dining in advance.

tourism_level.crowds: Peak during Fêtes de Genève weekend; otherwise moderate as locals leave on holiday and business travel pauses.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week; spikes around Fêtes de Genève weekend.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Festive, slightly emptier on weekdays, lake-focused throughout.

events_holidays.name: Swiss National Day (Fête Nationale)
events_holidays.description: Public holiday with parades, alpenhorn concerts, brunches on hill farms, and fireworks at lakefront villages.
events_holidays.notes: Most shops closed; restaurants busy. Trains and lake boats run holiday schedules.

events_holidays.name: Fêtes de Genève
events_holidays.description: Geneva's signature summer festival — food village, concerts, and the headline fireworks display over the harbour seen by hundreds of thousands.
events_holidays.notes: Book restaurants weeks ahead. Lakefront pedestrianised; tram diversions.

events_holidays.name: Assumption Day
events_holidays.description: Not a public holiday in Geneva but observed in some neighbouring French border areas; minor cross-border disruption.

unique_experiences.where: Lakefront — both shores, Quai du Mont-Blanc and Quai Gustave-Ador
unique_experiences.description: The signature pyrotechnic display: 60+ minutes choreographed to music, fired from barges in the harbour.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive 2–3 hours early for prime spots. Bains des Pâquis pier is excellent if reservation-free standing room remains.

unique_experiences.where: Hill farms near Geneva (e.g., Mont Salève farms, Jura alpages)
unique_experiences.description: Switzerland's farms open their doors for a traditional brunch — cheese, rösti, charcuterie, fresh bread.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weeks in advance via Schweizer Bauern. Bring cash.

unique_experiences.description: Watch the fireworks from the lake itself, on a CGN paddle-steamer or modern boat with dinner.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Books out months in advance. Confirm boarding times.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's classic open-air pool complex on the lake: Olympic pool, water slides, lake access, restaurants.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Arrive by 10:30 for shade. Day pass tickets at the gate.

weather.precipitation: Heat eases gradually; thunderstorms break the patterns.
weather.general_tips: Best mountain-clarity windows arrive late August. Mornings cool down faster — wonderful for runs and swims.

tourism_level.crowds: Calmer after Fêtes de Genève. End-of-month rentrée brings business and conference travel back.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft mid-week; weekend rates re-firming late month.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Lazy late summer, with peaks of energy on warm weekends.

events_holidays.name: Lake Parade
events_holidays.description: Electronic music parade along the lakefront, drawing tens of thousands.
events_holidays.notes: Check programme; large temporary stages and food vendors line the quais.

events_holidays.name: Late-Summer Vintage Begins
events_holidays.description: First Genevan grape harvest typically begins late month, with early-vintage tasting events at Mandement and Lavaux vineyards.
events_holidays.notes: Some cellar visits programmed; check Geneva Tourism.

unique_experiences.description: Late August evenings bring some of the clearest air of the year — ride up at 19:00 with a picnic for sunset over the entire Léman basin.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Check last cable car down time.

unique_experiences.description: If running, the parade brings stages and trucks along the quais — an end-of-summer street-festival atmosphere.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Check official programme. Tram detours likely.

unique_experiences.activity: Boat to Lausanne for the Day
unique_experiences.description: Long, scenic CGN cruise along the south shore to Lausanne — picnic, museum (Art Brut, Photo Elysée), and ride back at sunset.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Covered by Swiss Travel Pass. Bring a hat for top-deck sun.

unique_experiences.description: After much of Geneva returns from holiday, Carouge's terraces fill with end-of-summer apéro energy.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Reserve dinner at popular bistros.
